rationale: The cited paper is plausibly relevant because both papers center on uniqueness/conformity motives and social effects in conspicuous/public consumption contexts (citing note: need for uniqueness and public vs. private products; cited note: uniqueness/conformity in conspicuous-goods demand). However, the available match evidence is only a bare DOI reference with no in-text citation context, and the focal contributions differ (WOM behavior vs. competitive pricing model), so substantive use cannot be confirmed from current evidence.
